---
title: "gp_predict"
generated: true
description: "Per-element posterior mean and standard deviation of the GP at `x_new`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.gp`](../nitrix-stats-gp.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gp_predict -->
```python
gp_predict(result: GPResult, x_new: Float[Array, ' g'], *, parametric: Optional[Float[Array, 'g q']] = None, x_train: Optional[Float[Array, ' N']] = None, type: Literal['link', 'response'] = 'link') -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gp_predict -->
Per-element posterior mean and standard deviation of the GP at `x_new`.

Evaluates `mean = X_new beta` and the predictive variance
$\sigma_e^2 \operatorname{diag}(X_{new} V X_{new}^T)$, where the smooth columns of `X_new` are
the basis re-evaluated at `x_new`: for `engine='hsgp'` the (`rho`-
independent) eigenfunctions, reconstructed from the recorded domain
`(lo, hi, boundary)` and `rank` (self-contained); for `engine='exact'`
the Nystrom kernel features $K(x_{new}, x_{train}) U \Lambda^{-1/2}$, which need
the original `x_train` (the result does not store the shared training grid
among its per-element fields).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gp_predict -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `GPResult` | — | — | A `GPResult` from `gp_fit`. |
| `x_new` | `Float` | `g` | — | `(g,)` covariate grid to predict on. |
| `parametric` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'g q']]` | — | `None` | `(g, q)` parametric covariates matching `gp_fit`'s `parametric` (must be supplied when the fit used parametric columns). |
| `x_train` | `Optional[Float[Array, ' N']]` | — | `None` | `(N,)` original training covariate -- **required for `engine='exact'`** (ignored for `'hsgp'`). |
| `type` | `Literal['link', 'response']` | — | `'link'` | `'link'` (default) returns the latent linear-predictor `eta`; for a non-Gaussian fit, `'response'` maps the mean through `family.linkinv` (the mean response, e.g. a probability / rate) and scales `std` by the delta-method factor `\|d mu / d eta\|` (a point-estimate response-scale SD). A no-op for the Gaussian (identity-link) fit. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gp_predict -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `g` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
